title: Flavorful Malted German Pork Bowl
description: A romantic German dish featuring tender pork, malty flavors, and a glassy jelly sauce, all in a low-sodium and delicious package, served in an earthenware bowl.

ingredients.name: pork shoulder
ingredients.notes: scored with a sharp knife to create a fat layer on the surface

instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together the malt syrup, caraway seeds, black pepper, and salt-free seasoning blend.
Rub the mixture all over the scored pork shoulder, making sure to get some into the scored lines.
Place the pork shoulder in a large earthenware bowl and pour the dry white wine around it.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Remove the bowl from the refrigerator and let it sit at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Cover the bowl with aluminum foil and bake for 2 1/2 hours.
While the pork is baking, mix the gelatin and chicken broth in a small saucepan and heat over low heat, stirring until the gelatin is dissolved.
Remove the pork from the oven and brush the gelatin mixture all over the top of the pork.
Return the pork to the oven and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until the glaze is set and glassy.
Remove the pork from the oven and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley.
